summary: Diff implements a new frontend role-selection UI and adds a backend `User` type + `users` map, but it does not show the required `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` API, explicit `/select-role` routing/guards, or backend RBAC enforcement based on immutable `userType`. Frontend still uses Internet Identity and introduces an `initializeUser` flow with placeholder identity data instead of the specified Google-style token-based login method.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend exposes `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` that creates/updates a backend User (email/name/avatar) and leaves `userType` unset on first login; frontend provides a single "Continue with Google" login action and uses backend login flow.
missing_requirements.reason: Frontend login button text exists, but the diff shows Internet Identity `login()` being called and an `initializeUser(email,name,avatar)` mutation creating placeholder identity fields; no `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` method is evidenced in the backend diff summary.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend persists mapping from authenticated caller to User and `getCurrentUser()` returns caller’s User (or null if never logged in via Google flow).
missing_requirements.reason: Backend shows `User` type and `users` map, but the diff summary for `backend/main.mo` is truncated and does not evidence an implemented `getCurrentUser()` method or its null/not-logged-in behavior.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend implements `getCurrentUser()` and `setUserType(userType)` where `setUserType` is one-time only, rejects invalid values, and errors if already set.
missing_requirements.reason: Frontend calls `actor.setUserType(userType)`, but the backend diff excerpt does not show `setUserType` implementation, its one-time immutability enforcement, or invalid-value handling.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend enforces RBAC: player-only APIs reject team/null; team-only APIs reject player/null; role-required endpoints fail clearly when `userType` is null.
missing_requirements.reason: No backend endpoint changes are shown that gate existing player/team APIs based on stored immutable `userType`. The backend diff excerpt only shows type/state additions (User/UserType/users map).

missing_requirements.requirement: After login completes, app calls backend `getCurrentUser()` and routes: if `userType` is null redirect to `/select-role`; if player -> Player onboarding; if team -> Team onboarding; no role assigned during login.
missing_requirements.reason: AuthenticatedApp renders `RoleSelectionPage` when `currentUser.userType` is falsy, but there is no evidence of routing to the dedicated `/select-role` path. Also, the app auto-initializes a user record via `initializeUser` after identity login, which is not the specified loginWithGoogle-based flow.

missing_requirements.requirement: Dedicated `/select-role` route mandatory when `userType` is null with exactly two choices and permanence warning; selecting calls `setUserType` then routes to onboarding; on failure show error and re-fetch current user.
missing_requirements.reason: UI page exists with the two required button labels and permanence warning, and it calls `setUserType` with error display. However the diff does not evidence that it is reachable at `/select-role` (route definition not shown), nor the required behavior to re-fetch current user state on failure (it sets local error, but no explicit refetch is shown beyond react-query invalidation in the mutation onSuccess only).

missing_requirements.requirement: Routing guards: if authenticated and `userType` is null always redirect to `/select-role`; if `userType` is set prevent access to `/select-role` (redirect appropriately); backend state is source of truth.
missing_requirements.reason: AuthenticatedApp conditionally renders RoleSelectionPage inline when `userType` is unset, but there is no evidence of actual route guards/redirects involving the `/select-role` path (no router changes shown). Preventing access to `/select-role` when role is set also is not evidenced without explicit routing.

hallucinated_features.description: Introduced an `initializeUser(email,name,avatar)` backend API usage and automatic user initialization using Internet Identity principal-derived placeholder email/name and Dicebear avatar.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest specifies `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` and storing identity fields from that flow; it does not request an `initializeUser` endpoint or auto-generation of identity fields from principal for testing.

hallucinated_features.description: Implemented/continued Internet Identity-based login while labeling UI as "Continue with Google".
hallucinated_features.reason: Requirement asks for an MVP Google-style flow with a backend `loginWithGoogle(googleToken)` method; diff shows `useInternetIdentity().login()` used directly with no googleToken flow evidenced.
